title: Calder Atelier: Torque-Balanced Mobile with a Wide, Deep Silhouette
strategies: trivial: complete balanced tree over a fixed leaf order with minimal arms, collision-repaired by bumping rod scales (checker's reference baseline; one depth, spine-hugging silhouette)
greedy: chain leaves onto a spine and balance each rod with minimal integer arms -- 'just balance it', never spends the free rod-scale, so the silhouette stays cramped

strong: exploit that balance fixes only the arm RATIO -- scale each rod (aL,aR)=(t*wR/g,t*wL/g) up toward the arm ceiling to fling leaves into distinct horizontal bins, and group high-gcd/equal-weight subtrees so t can run large; pick a topology giving varied leaf depths; keep the best feasible construction
insight: the divisor structure of subtree weights (gcd) controls how far a rod can be scaled within [1,Amax], decoupling geometry from statics
